reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully captures the informal statement. Using `ℝ → ℝ` with `ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc a b)` and `f '' (Set.Icc a b) ⊆ Set.Ioi 0` is logically equivalent to "f : [a,b] → (0,∞) continuous" since values outside [a,b] don't appear in any hypothesis or conclusion. The integral over `Set.Ioo a b` equals the integral over `Set.Icc a b` for continuous functions (endpoints have measure zero), so `fgint` and `hI` correctly encode the required integral identities. The hypothesis `∃ x : Set.Icc a b, f x ≠ g x` correctly encodes "f ≠ g as functions on [a,b]". The conclusion `∀ n > 0, I (n+1) > I n` correctly states that $I_1 < I_2 < \cdots$ is strictly increasing, and `Tendsto I atTop atTop` is the standard Mathlib encoding of $\lim_{n\to\infty} I_n = \infty$ (independent of the unspecified value $I_0$). All components match.
